practical_worship_leadership: Classic worship opener; highly singable and energetic for all ages.
arrangement_tips: Full band with driving groove; electric guitar riff signature. Big final chorus.

title: Lamb of God
summary_theology: Worshipful meditation on Jesus as the Lamb of God who bears the sin of the world.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for communion or Good Friday. Very intimate and reverent.
arrangement_tips: Soft piano or acoustic; restrained. Choir on the refrain adds depth.

title: El Shaddai
summary_theology: Meditation on God's covenant name El Shaddai, the God Almighty who fulfills every promise.
practical_worship_leadership: Educational and worshipful; works in a series on the names of God.
arrangement_tips: Piano ballad; can add Hebrew phrases. Congregation may need to learn it first.

title: Via Dolorosa
summary_theology: Dramatic narration of Christ's journey to Calvary, emphasizing the weight of sin he bore.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for Good Friday dramatic worship or Stations of the Cross.
arrangement_tips: Orchestral arrangement; suited for a strong soloist. Congregation listens and meditates.

title: Find Us Faithful
summary_theology: Prayer for God to find each generation faithful, leaving a legacy of faith for those who follow.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for legacy and generational focus. Effective at ordinations and installations.
arrangement_tips: Ballad feel; piano or full orchestra. Choir adds richness to the chorus.

title: Offering
summary_theology: Presents worship itself as an offering, a sacrifice of praise given to God in response to his greatness.
practical_worship_leadership: Works beautifully during offering time or as a dedicated song of consecration.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led; acoustic and worship-leader feel. Simple enough for any size church.

title: Your Name
summary_theology: Declares the supremacy of the name of Jesus as the hope and highest praise of all creation.
practical_worship_leadership: Accessible worship song; good for all ages and congregational sizes.
arrangement_tips: Full band or acoustic; bright and joyful. Easy chorus that congregation picks up fast.

title: Everlasting God
summary_theology: Declares God's eternal power that renews the weary, based on Isaiah's promise of soaring on wings like eagles.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy; great opener or mid-set climax. Very popular across denominations.
arrangement_tips: Electric guitar riff drives the song; full band. Congregation knows it quickly.

title: Yes and Amen
summary_theology: Declares that every promise of God is yes and amen in Christ, fueling confident faith.
practical_worship_leadership: Strong bridge for extended worship or declaration. Congregation engages deeply.
arrangement_tips: Builds from sparse keys to full band. Long bridge section allows extended declaration.

title: Ever Be
summary_theology: Pledges unending praise to God whose love and faithfulness endure forever and never fail.
practical_worship_leadership: Meditative and soaking; good for extended worship or as a final song of response.
arrangement_tips: Lush piano and strings; build slowly. Use space and silence for maximum impact.

title: Glorious Day
summary_theology: Celebrates the glorious day of resurrection and the transformation it brings to those who trust in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Easter favourite; high energy. Can be used year-round as a resurrection celebration.
arrangement_tips: Full rock production; electric guitar hook. Big anthem feel.

title: Living Hope
summary_theology: Declares the resurrection of Christ as the living hope that sustains through every trial and into eternity.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the best Easter anthems of the past decade. Easy to lead and powerful to sing.
arrangement_tips: Anthemic; start acoustic then full electric. Big finish.

title: Even So Come
summary_theology: Eschatological prayer song expressing longing for Christ's return and the fullness of God's kingdom.
practical_worship_leadership: Effective for Advent services or any eschatological theme.
arrangement_tips: Piano and strings; build to full band. Allow the congregation to reflect.

title: Yet Not I but Through Christ in Me
summary_theology: Theologically rich modern hymn tracing the believer's journey through suffering to glory by union with Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Profound; may need teaching. Rewards repeated singing. Use in a series on union with Christ.
arrangement_tips: Three-verse hymn structure; piano or guitar. Do not rush. Congregation learns verse by verse.

title: Leaning on the Everlasting Arms
summary_theology: Joyful expression of security found in leaning entirely on God's everlasting arms of support.
practical_worship_leadership: Very accessible; wide generational appeal. Can be used in hymn sings or worship.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat piano march; can add banjo or acoustic instruments. Great for intergenerational services.

title: Softly and Tenderly Jesus Is Calling
summary_theology: Evangelistic invitation hymn presenting Christ's gentle, persistent call to the wandering sinner.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic altar-call hymn; still very effective for invitation moments.
arrangement_tips: Piano only; gentle waltz. Soft and tender — match the title. Let it breathe.

title: Wonderful Words of Life
summary_theology: Classic gospel hymn celebrating the life-giving words of Christ and Scripture.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for Bible Sunday or any service celebrating Scripture. Very singable chorus.
arrangement_tips: Bright piano; lively tempo. Can add light percussion.

title: I Will Sing of My Redeemer
summary_theology: Exuberant praise of the Redeemer and the power of his atoning love, sung from a heart of gratitude.
practical_worship_leadership: Joyful and accessible; good for traditional-leaning or blended services.
arrangement_tips: March-like piano; full congregation. Four-part harmony works beautifully.

title: More Love to Thee O Christ
summary_theology: Personal prayer growing in love for Christ above every earthly consolation.
practical_worship_leadership: Intimate and reflective; good for prayer services or quiet moments of devotion.
arrangement_tips: Piano or acoustic guitar; gentle and restrained. Congregation should sing softly.

title: I Heard the Voice of Jesus Say
summary_theology: Evangelical dialogue hymn with Christ offering rest, water, and light to the weary soul.
practical_worship_leadership: Three rich verse-response stanzas; effective for teaching or evangelism focus.
arrangement_tips: Waltz feel; traditional tune Vox Dilecti or third tune. Gentle and inviting.

title: Baruch Hashem Adonai
summary_theology: Hebrew praise declaration Blessed be the name of the Lord, rooted in Jewish worship tradition fulfilled in Christ.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for Messianic-flavored services or teaching on the Jewish roots of Christian faith.
arrangement_tips: Middle-Eastern percussion, violin, acoustic instruments. Joyful dance feel.

title: Jerusalem
summary_theology: Celebration of Jerusalem as God's holy city and the place of his promises, connected to Christ's redemption.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for Israel-focused prayer nights or Messianic congregations.
arrangement_tips: Israeli folk feel; violin, percussion, dance rhythms.

title: Shema (Hear O Israel)
summary_theology: The foundational Jewish declaration of God's oneness, quoted by Jesus as the greatest commandment.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for teaching context or interfaith dialogue. Grounds the church in its Hebrew heritage.
arrangement_tips: Can be sung in Hebrew or English; simple melody with optional instrumentation.

title: The Warrior Is a Child
summary_theology: Honest admission of the warrior-heart that also needs to run to God as a child for rest and restoration.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply personal and tender; works well in leadership retreats or care contexts.
arrangement_tips: Ballad; piano only. Needs a strong expressive vocalist. Minimize production.

title: Lift Him Up
summary_theology: Call to lift Jesus high in worship, believing his promise that if lifted up he will draw all people.
practical_worship_leadership: Highly energetic congregational song; great for evangelistic-flavored services.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and full choir; march feel. Very accessible refrain.

title: Ancient of Days
summary_theology: Worship song drawing from Daniel's vision of the Ancient of Days, declaring God's eternal sovereignty.
practical_worship_leadership: Majestic and weighty; works well as a climactic worship moment.
arrangement_tips: Full orchestra feel or gospel choir; build from quiet to massive.

title: The Great Adventure
summary_theology: Celebrates the Christian life as an adventure of following Jesus wherever he leads.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for youth contexts or a discipleship series. High energy and exciting.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock feel; guitar-driven. Congregation may need projection for the verses.

title: God Is God
summary_theology: Honest wrestling with suffering that arrives at trust in God's sovereign purposes beyond human understanding.
practical_worship_leadership: Deep song for grief contexts, loss, or theodicy discussions. Requires pastoral sensitivity.
arrangement_tips: Piano ballad; understated. Let the congregation sit with the words.

title: I Stand in Awe
summary_theology: Reflective worship song expressing wonder at the ineffable holiness and beauty of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Slows a service down into reverent awe; good before prayer or preaching.
arrangement_tips: Piano; sparse and reverent. Do not over-produce.

title: How Good It Is
summary_theology: Celebrates the goodness and beauty of Christian community gathered in worship.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for church anniversary or body-life themed services.
arrangement_tips: Bright and joyful; acoustic or full band. Congregation picks it up easily.

title: Blessed Be the Lord God Almighty
summary_theology: Classic praise chorus of adoration to God Almighty, Father and Son, drawing from Revelation.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic that older worshipers know; introduce to younger generations as a foundation.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full congregation; march feel. Add choir for richness.

title: The Lord Reigns
summary_theology: Jubilant praise declaring that the Lord is king over all the earth forever.
practical_worship_leadership: Energetic and simple; great for opening a celebration service.
arrangement_tips: March-like piano; add drums. Very easy for congregation to learn.

title: Break Every Chain
summary_theology: Declaration of the power in the name of Jesus to break every chain of bondage.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for freedom-themed services or altar ministry. Repetition builds declaration.
arrangement_tips: Build slowly; sparse piano to full electric. The repeated chorus becomes corporate declaration.

title: Set a Fire
summary_theology: Prayer for a holy fire of the Spirit to be set in the heart that no human can extinguish.
practical_worship_leadership: Soaking and meditative; allows extended quiet prayer. Use in prayer ministry settings.
arrangement_tips: Very sparse; electric guitar with reverb, piano. No percussion necessary.

title: It Is Well
summary_theology: Modern setting of the classic hymn, maintaining its theology of soul-peace rooted in Christ's redemption.
practical_worship_leadership: Works in grief contexts or as a response song after difficult teaching.
arrangement_tips: Piano and acoustic; can build to full band. Familiar text eases congregation into it.

title: Worthy of It All
summary_theology: Soaking worship song declaring God worthy of all surrender and offering the whole life in response.
practical_worship_leadership: Extremely effective in extended worship; allows deep ministry and prayer.
arrangement_tips: Very sparse — piano or guitar only. Can repeat for 15-20 minutes as soaking worship.

title: Abide
summary_theology: Invitation to abide deeply in Christ, resting in him as the source of all fruit and life.
practical_worship_leadership: Quiet and intimate; works in a Lord's Supper context or prayer service.
arrangement_tips: Piano or acoustic guitar only; very simple. Allow silence between phrases.

title: Man of Your Word
summary_theology: Testifies to God's faithfulness to his word and covenant, proven through his people's history.
practical_worship_leadership: Strong for testimonial services or covenant renewal. Very accessible groove.
arrangement_tips: Gospel-soul blend; piano, organ, choir. Allow congregation to respond with testimonies.

title: Might of Your Love
summary_theology: Declares the incomprehensible might of God's love as the driving force of all worship.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy opener; electric guitar-led with full production.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock production; driving rhythm. Strong chorus for congregation to lock onto.

title: Joyful
summary_theology: Exuberant celebration of the joy found in salvation, calling the whole congregation to rejoice.
practical_worship_leadership: Great opener for a celebration service; high energy, very participatory.
arrangement_tips: Full band production; dance groove. Congregation should feel freedom to move.

title: I Am Not Afraid
summary_theology: Declaration of faith-based courage, trusting in God's strength rather than succumbing to fear.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on faith, anxiety, or courage. Congregation engages enthusiastically.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary rock; anthemic. Build to a full, confident final chorus.

title: Great Is Your Faithfulness
summary_theology: Contemporary setting of the classic text celebrating God's new mercies every morning.
practical_worship_leadership: Bridges generations; familiar text with fresh arrangement. Very accessible.
arrangement_tips: Piano-led with optional full band. The original hymn can alternate with this arrangement.

title: O Praise the Name (Anastasis)
summary_theology: New hymn narrating the death and resurrection of Christ and calling forth universal praise.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for Easter or communion. Congregations respond deeply to this song.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and strings; do not overpower the lyrics. The melody carries the congregation.

title: Hymn of Heaven
summary_theology: Anticipates the eternal hymn of heaven and joins the song now in faith, declaring what is to come.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for any service; the eschatological framing gives it special depth.
arrangement_tips: Contemporary production; anthemic. The pre-chorus and chorus are extremely singable.

title: Alas and Did My Savior Bleed
summary_theology: Watts' classic of astonishment at the cross, that the King of glory would die for sinners.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for communion or a cross-centered service. Powerful older hymn.
arrangement_tips: Can use Martyrdom tune; simple piano. Or the Chris Rice modern setting.

title: Jesus Shall Reign
summary_theology: Watts' Psalm 72 paraphrase declaring Christ's global, eternal reign over all nations.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for missions Sunday or World Communion Sunday. Strong close.
arrangement_tips: March tune Duke Street; organ and brass for maximum impact.

title: Lord Speak to Me
summary_theology: Prayer to be used by God to reach others, teaching and speaking what God gives.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for ordinations, teacher installations, or a service on calling and ministry.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano; gentle and reflective. Congregation should sing as a corporate prayer.

title: O God Our Help in Ages Past
summary_theology: Majestic psalm paraphrase contrasting the brevity of human life with God's eternal shelter.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for New Year services, national days, or remembrance events.
arrangement_tips: Tune St. Anne; organ and congregation. Stately, unhurried.

title: Now Thank We All Our God
summary_theology: German Reformation thanksgiving hymn written in the midst of plague, declaring blessing from God.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic Thanksgiving or harvest festival hymn. Very full congregation sound.
arrangement_tips: Organ and brass; march feel. Can be done a cappella for dramatic effect.

title: O Jesus I Have Promised
summary_theology: Covenant hymn of commitment to follow Jesus wherever he leads, asking for his help to remain faithful.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for confirmation or dedication services; any service of public commitment.
arrangement_tips: Traditional hymn tune; piano or organ. Stately and purposeful.

title: Sweet Hour of Prayer
summary_theology: Celebrates the blessing of unhurried time in prayer, presenting every need and burden to God.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for prayer emphasis weekends or to begin a corporate prayer time.
arrangement_tips: Gentle piano or acoustic; soft and reflective. Allow lingering at the end.

title: My Faith Looks Up to Thee
summary_theology: Hymn of devoted faith looking to the Lamb of Calvary for cleansing, strength, and guidance.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for communion or a service on faith. Older congregations will know it.
arrangement_tips: Tune Olivet; piano accompaniment. Quiet and devotional.

title: Same Power
summary_theology: Declares that the same resurrection power that raised Christ lives in every believer through the Spirit.
practical_worship_leadership: High energy; great for services on the Holy Spirit or overcoming.
arrangement_tips: Rock production; driving guitar riff. Congregation shouts the chorus.

title: Rise Up
summary_theology: Call to rise above despair through the life of Christ who conquered death and gives new life.
practical_worship_leadership: Uplifting and accessible; works for youth and young adults. Country-pop feel.
arrangement_tips: Country-influenced contemporary; acoustic, electric, full band. Very catchy.

title: Never Been a Moment
summary_theology: Personal testimony that there has never been a moment when God was not faithful and present.
practical_worship_leadership: Great after a testimony segment; very singable and authentic.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic feel; country-influenced. Piano and guitar work well together.

title: What a Friend We Have in Jesus
summary_theology: Classic hymn encouraging believers to bring every burden to Jesus in prayer rather than carrying it alone.
practical_worship_leadership: Universally known; great for prayer services or pastoral care services.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano; or use the Marc James modern arrangement. Congregation knows every word.

title: Beautiful One
summary_theology: Worship song meditating on the incomparable beauty and wonder of who Jesus is.
practical_worship_leadership: Works in many contexts; congregation-friendly melody. Tim Hughes classic.
arrangement_tips: Piano and acoustic guitar; can build to full band. Key changes work well.

title: Consuming Fire
summary_theology: Prayer for the Holy Spirit as consuming fire to purify and ignite the church for God's glory.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy worship; works for revival or pentecost services.
arrangement_tips: Rock arrangement; guitar-driven. Build to a climactic chorus.

title: Lord of Lords
summary_theology: Anthemic declaration of Christ as Lord of lords and King of kings over all creation and history.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful declaration song; congregation voices this as a corporate affirmation.
arrangement_tips: Full production; electric guitar, full band. Very singable anthem.

title: Salvation Belongs to Our God
summary_theology: Draws from the great multitude of Revelation 7, declaring salvation belongs to God and the Lamb alone.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic contemporary worship anthem; congregations across traditions sing it.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full band; march-like. Simple enough for any size congregation.

title: Step by Step
summary_theology: Simple daily commitment to follow God step by step, seeking him in every moment.
practical_worship_leadership: Very accessible; youth and adults love it. Good for beginning of a year or new season.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic guitar or piano; can go full band. The congregation joins the chorus immediately.

title: Here I Am Lord
summary_theology: Liturgical song of vocation, answering the divine call with willingness to go and serve.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic for ordinations, commissioning, and mission Sundays.
arrangement_tips: Piano and congregation; optional orchestra. Works in Catholic and Protestant contexts.

title: On Eagles Wings
summary_theology: Psalm 91-based liturgical song of comfort, declaring God's protective care over his beloved.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved for funerals and grief services as well as general worship.
arrangement_tips: Piano and soloist; or congregation. Gentle, soaring melody.

title: See the Conqueror Mounts in Triumph
summary_theology: Ascension hymn celebrating Christ's triumphant entry into heaven as conqueror and king.
practical_worship_leadership: Best for Ascension Sunday. Helps congregations understand the importance of Christ's ascension.
arrangement_tips: Tune In Babilone; march feel. Full organ and congregation.

title: Lift Every Voice and Sing
summary_theology: The Black National Anthem, expressing the journey of faith through suffering toward hope and freedom.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for MLK Sunday, Black History Month, or justice-themed services.
arrangement_tips: Piano and choir; can add full band. Four-part harmony is beautiful. Stand to sing.

title: This Little Light of Mine
summary_theology: Simple declaration of intent to let the light of Christ shine through the believer's life everywhere.
practical_worship_leadership: Intergenerational; children love it. Great for an outreach or mission Sunday.
arrangement_tips: Upbeat and joyful; piano and guitar. Can add handclaps and percussion.

title: I Will Rise
summary_theology: Hope-filled song of resurrection faith, declaring confident expectation of rising to eternal life.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for funerals or Easter. Very accessible and emotionally resonant.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and electric blend; builds to an anthemic finish. Simple enough for all.

title: Lord I Lift Your Name on High
summary_theology: Simple but complete gospel summary in song — Christ came from heaven, died, and rose for us.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved classic; even unchurched visitors often know this song. Great for evangelistic contexts.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic or full band; upbeat and accessible. Key of G works for most voices.

title: All Creatures of Our God and King
summary_theology: St. Francis hymn calling all creation to praise God, given new voice through W.H. Draper's translation.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for creation care themes, Harvest Sunday, or any service on the Creator.
arrangement_tips: Alleluia refrain is key; can build verse by verse. Full congregation and organ.

title: Great Is Thy Faithfulness
summary_theology: The great hymn of God's faithfulness across every season, all nature and provision singing his constancy.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most beloved hymns ever written; works for any service at any time.
arrangement_tips: Waltz feel; piano or organ. Can do solo verse with congregation chorus. Any arrangement works.

title: Immortal Invisible God Only Wise
summary_theology: Majestic hymn of adoration declaring God's immortal, transcendent, glorious nature beyond human comprehension.
practical_worship_leadership: Strong opening hymn; especially for Trinity Sunday or a series on God's attributes.
arrangement_tips: Tune St. Denio; organ and brass. Full congregation four-part harmony.

title: Praise Him Praise Him
summary_theology: Joyful proclamation of praise to Jesus, hailing him as the Lamb of God and mighty Savior.
practical_worship_leadership: Lively classic; great for intergenerational worship or a joyful celebration service.
arrangement_tips: Bright march piano; can add percussion and brass. Very easy refrain.

title: Be Thou My Vision
summary_theology: Ancient Irish prayer hymn placing God as the supreme vision and treasure above all earthly things.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most beloved hymns globally; works in virtually any setting.
arrangement_tips: Tune Slane; waltz feel. Can be done a cappella, acoustic, or full production.

title: Praise the Lord Ye Heavens Adore Him
summary_theology: Creation hymn calling every tier of existence to offer praise to the Lord of all creation.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for Harvest, creation-themed services, or general celebration Sundays.
arrangement_tips: Tune Hyfrydol or Austria; full organ and congregation. March feel.

title: We Three Kings
summary_theology: Epiphany carol tracing the journey of the magi and the symbolic meaning of each gift for Christ's person and work.
practical_worship_leadership: Best for Epiphany season or the Twelve Days of Christmas. Dramatic and teachable.
arrangement_tips: Minor verse, major refrain. Full congregation or small choir. Keep the minor feel on verses.

title: That's Why We Praise Him
summary_theology: Joyful declaration of the reason behind all praise: Jesus, his name, and his saving work.
practical_worship_leadership: Great opener; easy for all ages. Very direct and congregationally accessible.
arrangement_tips: Piano and full band; bright and celebratory. Add clapping on 2 and 4.

title: In the Secret
summary_theology: Intimate Vineyard song expressing deep longing to know God in the hidden place of communion.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for prayer services or quiet mid-service worship. Deeply personal.
arrangement_tips: Very sparse; acoustic guitar or keys only. Allow space and silence.

title: Every Praise
summary_theology: Declares that every expression of praise belongs to God alone as the source of all blessing.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic gospel praise song; choir-and-congregation call-and-response works beautifully.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and choir; simple structure that builds naturally.

title: I Need You to Survive
summary_theology: Declares the body of Christ's mutual dependence and the call to love one another as a survival need.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful for church unity services or small group worship. Very community-focused.
arrangement_tips: Gospel feel; choir and congregation together. Slow and reflective.

title: Intentional
summary_theology: Declares that every detail of life is under God's intentional care and purpose, even hard things.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for services on suffering or divine purpose. Congregation resonates deeply.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and choir; builds from sparse to full. Allow extended bridge.

title: The Solid Rock (My Hope Is Built)
summary_theology: Declares Christ alone as the solid rock on which the believer builds all hope, renouncing every other ground.
practical_worship_leadership: Beloved classic; great for a foundation-of-faith series or baptism Sunday.
arrangement_tips: Traditional tune Solid Rock; piano or organ. Very singable by all generations.

title: Stand Up Stand Up for Jesus
summary_theology: March hymn calling soldiers of the cross to stand firm in spiritual battle for Christ's glory.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for spiritual warfare series or any service calling for courage.
arrangement_tips: March tune Webb; drums, brass, full congregation standing.

title: Beneath the Cross of Jesus
summary_theology: Meditation on the cross as the believer's only refuge, shade, and resting place from all self-reliance.
practical_worship_leadership: Deeply reflective; perfect for Lent or communion. Very moving when sung slowly.
arrangement_tips: Piano alone; sing very slowly and carefully. Each stanza is a gem.

title: I Love to Tell the Story
summary_theology: Classic missionary hymn expressing the joy of telling the old, old story of Jesus and his love.
practical_worship_leadership: Good for evangelism-focused services. The chorus is very familiar.
arrangement_tips: March feel; piano and congregation. Very accessible and joyful.

title: The Church's One Foundation
summary_theology: Declares Jesus Christ as the one foundation of the universal church, baptized in his name and blood.
practical_worship_leadership: Excellent for church anniversaries, ecumenical services, or a series on the church.
arrangement_tips: Tune Aurelia; organ and four-part congregation. Stately and confident.

title: Glorious Things of Thee Are Spoken
summary_theology: Newton's hymn celebrating the glorious privileges of belonging to Zion, the city of God.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for church anniversary or any service celebrating the community of faith.
arrangement_tips: Tune Austria; organ and full congregation. Majestic and celebratory.

title: There's a Wideness in God's Mercy
summary_theology: Celebrates the vast, oceanic mercy of God that exceeds all human narrowness or hard-heartedness.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for a sermon on grace or mercy; particularly good for contexts of receiving outcasts.
arrangement_tips: Tune In Babilone; piano or organ. Gentle and warm.

title: God Moves in a Mysterious Way
summary_theology: Cowper's great hymn of trust in God's inscrutable ways, encouraging faith when providence seems dark.
practical_worship_leadership: Particularly moving for services dealing with suffering, loss, or unanswered questions.
arrangement_tips: Simple piano; slow and reflective. The words do all the work.

title: Come We That Love the Lord
summary_theology: Joyful call for God's people to unite in worship and march together toward the heavenly Zion.
practical_worship_leadership: Very joyful and energetic; great for an upbeat opening or community celebration.
arrangement_tips: March tune Marching to Zion; piano and percussion. Add handclaps.

title: When the Roll Is Called Up Yonder
summary_theology: Anticipatory hymn of the final roll call of God's people at the resurrection, expressing joy at being present.
practical_worship_leadership: Lively and energetic; familiar to many. Good for All Saints or resurrection-themed services.
arrangement_tips: March-like piano; lively tempo. Congregation usually knows the chorus.

title: Living for Jesus
summary_theology: Hymn of total consecration committing each day to living in Christ's service and surrender.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for a discipleship series or any service of recommitment.
arrangement_tips: Bright march tempo; piano and congregation. Chorus is easy to learn.

title: At the Cross
summary_theology: Watts' meditation on the cross with Hudson's refrain, marveling that Christ would die for such a sinner.
practical_worship_leadership: Classic for communion or Good Friday. The refrain is instantly memorable.
arrangement_tips: Piano with four-part harmony; refrain swells naturally. Contemporary arrangements also work.

title: O for a Thousand Tongues to Sing
summary_theology: Wesley's anniversary hymn of praise, longing for more capacity to declare the glories of his Savior.
practical_worship_leadership: Traditional Methodist opener; works for any celebratory service. Universally beloved.
arrangement_tips: Tune Azmon; organ and congregation. Can add brass for special occasions.

title: Rejoice the Lord Is King
summary_theology: Easter and Ascension hymn commanding joyful celebration because the risen Christ is now enthroned as King.
practical_worship_leadership: Perfect for Ascension Sunday or Easter season. Very joyful and energetic.
arrangement_tips: Tune Darwall; full organ and brass. March-like. The Lift up your heart refrain is joyful.

title: We Have Overcome
summary_theology: Declaration of the believer's victory through the blood of the Lamb and word of testimony.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful declaration song; great at the end of a spiritual warfare or freedom service.
arrangement_tips: Full gospel-soul production; keys, bass, drums. Extended outro for spontaneous declaration.

title: Raise a Hallelujah
summary_theology: Declares praise as a weapon in spiritual battle, lifting hallelujah even in the most difficult moments.
practical_worship_leadership: Extremely popular; congregation loves the anthemic quality. Works in most contexts.
arrangement_tips: Build from acoustic to full electric. Final chorus should feel like a battle cry.

title: Nothing Is Impossible
summary_theology: Declares bold faith that nothing is impossible for God, calling believers to trust for the miraculous.
practical_worship_leadership: High-energy; great for faith-healing or revival services. Australian charismatic style.
arrangement_tips: Full electric production; driving rhythm section. Very anthemic chorus.

title: Open Heaven / River Wild
summary_theology: Prayer for heaven to open and the Spirit to pour out like a wild river over the congregation.
practical_worship_leadership: Works for Spirit-focused services or prayer for revival. Very energetic.
arrangement_tips: Full electric production; create wave-like dynamics. Allow spontaneous moments.

title: Hallelujah (Praise the Lord)
summary_theology: Gospel anthem of pure praise, calling forth the hallelujah of the redeemed before the throne.
practical_worship_leadership: Powerful choir anthem; also works with strong worship band and congregation.
arrangement_tips: Gospel piano and choir; build gradually. The hallelujah section allows extended worship.

title: New Season
summary_theology: Declares the arrival of a new season of God's blessing and fresh move, releasing the old and embracing the new.
practical_worship_leadership: Great for New Year services, church anniversaries, or transition-themed services.
arrangement_tips: Gospel rhythm section; full band and choir. Energetic and celebratory.

title: Goodness of God
summary_theology: Testimonial declaration that God has been good through every season, grounding worship in lived experience.
practical_worship_leadership: One of the most widely used modern worship songs. Works in virtually any context.
arrangement_tips: Acoustic and intimate start; build to full production. Final chorus is anthemic.
